project.name: Car Insurance Comparison Market
project.description: End-to-end operational and technical map of a car insurance comparison website, from customer data entry through quote generation to results display, including downstream data consumers.

nodes.label: Customer
nodes.description: Individual seeking car insurance quotes via the comparison site.

nodes.label: Enter Details
nodes.description: Customer provides personal information, vehicle details, and cover preferences across a multi-step form.

nodes.label: Reg Lookup
nodes.description: Customer enters vehicle registration plate, triggering auto-population of vehicle details from an external data provider.

nodes.label: Comparison Frontend
nodes.description: Customer-facing web and mobile application for the comparison site.

nodes.label: Comparison Backend
nodes.description: API layer handling form submissions, enrichment orchestration, quote distribution, and session management.

nodes.label: Vehicle Data Provider
nodes.description: Third-party service returning vehicle details from a registration plate (e.g. HPI, CDL). Returns make, model, year, engine size, fuel type, insurance group, estimated value.

nodes.label: Customer Profile
nodes.description: Name, date of birth, address, licence number, employment status, driving history declarations.

nodes.label: Vehicle Data
nodes.description: Registration, make, model, year, engine size, fuel type, insurance group, estimated value.

nodes.label: Cover Preferences
nodes.description: Desired start date, cover type (comprehensive / third party / third party fire and theft), voluntary excess, add-on preferences.

nodes.label: Session Database
nodes.description: Stores the in-progress quote request against a session or anonymous identifier.

openQuestions: How long are sessions retained?
Are incomplete sessions purged or archived?

nodes.label: Enrichment Orchestration
nodes.description: Backend coordinates parallel calls to external data providers to enrich the customer's declared information before generating quotes.

nodes.label: Claims History Check
nodes.description: Customer's declared claims history is verified against the industry shared claims database (CUE).

nodes.label: Licence Validation
nodes.description: Customer's licence details are verified against DVLA records, confirming status, entitlements, endorsements, and penalty points.

nodes.label: Address Verification
nodes.description: Customer's declared address is verified against a third-party address database.

nodes.label: Fraud Scoring
nodes.description: Pre-quote fraud risk assessment based on the enriched customer profile.

openQuestions: Which provider is actually used?
Is this a hard gate or just a flag for downstream underwriters?

nodes.label: Claims Discrepancy Check
nodes.description: Do the claims returned by CUE match what the customer declared? If not, the customer must confirm or explain before quotes can proceed.

nodes.label: Resolve Discrepancy
nodes.description: Customer is presented with the claims discrepancy and asked to confirm, amend, or explain before the journey can continue.

nodes.label: CUE
nodes.description: Claims and Underwriting Exchange — shared industry database of motor claims history, operated by the Motor Insurers' Bureau.

nodes.label: MyLicence / DVLA
nodes.description: DVLA licence validation service returning licence status, endorsements, penalty points, and entitlements.

nodes.label: Address Verification Service
nodes.description: Third-party address lookup and verification service (e.g. Loqate, Experian QAS).

nodes.label: Fraud Scoring Service
nodes.description: Third-party fraud risk scoring engine (e.g. LexisNexis, Synectics). Consumes enriched personal data and returns a risk score.

openQuestions: Which provider is actually used?
Is this a hard gate or just a flag for downstream underwriters?

nodes.label: Claims History
nodes.description: Verified claims records returned from CUE — claim dates, types, fault status, amounts.

nodes.label: Licence Record
nodes.description: Verified licence details — status, categories, endorsements, penalty points, disqualifications.

nodes.label: Fraud Score
nodes.description: Numerical risk score and flag indicators from fraud screening.

nodes.label: Enriched Quote Request
nodes.description: The final assembled object combining customer-declared data, vehicle data, cover preferences, and all enrichment results. This is what gets sent to insurers.

nodes.label: Pending Verification
nodes.description: Quote request is paused because enrichment data conflicts with customer declarations.

nodes.label: Verified
nodes.description: All enrichment checks complete, discrepancies resolved, quote request ready for distribution.

nodes.label: Quote Distribution
nodes.description: The enriched quote request is packaged into a standardised format and sent simultaneously to the insurer panel.

nodes.label: Quote Engine
nodes.description: Internal service managing the fan-out to insurers — handles request formatting, parallel dispatch, timeout management, and response collection.

nodes.label: Insurer A — QuoteFast Insurance
nodes.description: Representative insurer that returns a successful quote. Stands in for the ~20 insurers on the panel.

nodes.label: Insurer B — SecureGuard Insurance
nodes.description: Representative insurer that declines to quote.

nodes.label: Insurer C — BudgetCover Insurance
nodes.description: Representative insurer that times out without responding.

openQuestions: Is there monitoring on insurer response times?
Are persistently slow insurers flagged or removed from the panel?

nodes.label: Standardised Quote Request
nodes.description: The enriched customer data reformatted into the common schema expected by insurer APIs.

nodes.label: Quote Response — QuoteFast
nodes.description: Successful quote — annual premium, cover details, excess, included features, optional add-ons, quote reference.

nodes.label: Decline Response — SecureGuard
nodes.description: Decline notification — insurer chose not to quote. May include a reason code.

nodes.label: Timeout — BudgetCover
nodes.description: No response received from BudgetCover Insurance within the timeout window.

nodes.label: Response Evaluation
nodes.description: For each insurer — did they return a quote, a decline, or nothing within the timeout window?

nodes.label: Quotes Collected
nodes.description: All insurer responses received or timed out. The system is ready to process and display results.

nodes.label: Quotes Database
nodes.description: Stores all insurer responses — successful quotes, declines, and timeout records — linked to the customer session.

nodes.label: Response Normalisation
nodes.description: Successful quotes are transformed into a consistent display format — premium, cover level, excess, key features, insurer brand, add-on options.

nodes.label: Ranking
nodes.description: Normalised quotes are sorted for display. Primary sort is price, but commercial weighting may also apply — commission rates, preferred partnerships, promotional placements.

openQuestions: Does ranking factor in commission?
Are there paid placements?
How transparent is this to the customer?

nodes.label: Display Results
nodes.description: Customer sees the ranked list of quotes and can browse, compare, and select.

nodes.label: Ranked Quote List
nodes.description: The ordered set of quotes ready for display, with normalised fields for comparison.

nodes.label: Results Presented
nodes.description: Customer is viewing available quotes. Journey is complete from the comparison site's perspective unless the customer selects a quote for referral.

nodes.label: Event Logging
nodes.description: Every significant action in the journey — form submissions, enrichment results, insurer responses, customer interactions — is logged as an event for downstream consumption.

nodes.label: Data Pipeline
nodes.description: ETL/ELT process moving event and transactional data from operational databases into the data warehouse.

nodes.label: Data Warehouse
nodes.description: Central analytical store containing quote journeys, enrichment data, insurer responses, conversion events, and session metadata.

openQuestions: What PII retention policy applies to customer journey data?
Is there automated purging?

nodes.label: Analytics Dashboard
nodes.description: Business intelligence dashboards consumed by product, commercial, and operations teams — conversion funnels, quote volumes, insurer performance, drop-off analysis, average premiums.

nodes.label: Conversion Metrics
nodes.description: Key performance indicators — quote-to-click-through rate, stage drop-off rates, average quotes per session, insurer win rates.

nodes.label: Abandoned Journey Detection
nodes.description: Identifies customers who received quotes but did not click through to an insurer within a defined time window.

openQuestions: What is the abandonment time window?
Is this real-time or batch?

nodes.label: CRM / Marketing Platform
nodes.description: Marketing automation system that triggers re-engagement campaigns based on abandoned journey signals.

openQuestions: What consent model governs re-engagement emails?
Is consent captured at form entry or assumed?
Is PII pushed to CRM or does it pull from the warehouse?

nodes.label: Re-engagement Email
nodes.description: Automated email sent to customers who abandoned after receiving quotes — may include a link back to their saved results.

nodes.label: Finance Team Member
nodes.description: Person in the finance team responsible for monthly commission reconciliation.

nodes.label: Commission Reconciliation
nodes.description: Finance team manually reconciles insurer commission statements against internal referral records to verify payments.

openQuestions: How often does this break?
Is there a plan to automate?

nodes.label: Commission Spreadsheet
nodes.description: Excel workbook maintained by the finance team, pulling data from the warehouse export and matching against insurer PDF statements.

openQuestions: How many people can edit this?
Is there version control?
What happens if it corrupts?

nodes.label: Commission Data Export
nodes.description: CSV or Excel export pulled from the data warehouse containing referral volumes, click-throughs, and attributed sales per insurer.

edges.label: CUE claims compared against declared claims

metadata.condition: Discrepancy between declared and verified claims

openQuestions: Is PII pushed to CRM or does CRM pull?
What consent model applies?

edges.label: Finance copies data into reconciliation workbook

views.label: Journey View
views.description: Operational story of the customer quote journey — actors, processes, decisions, states, and outcomes.

views.label: Technical Architecture
views.description: Systems, integrations, and infrastructure that implement the operational flow.

views.label: Data Lineage
views.description: How data objects are created, transformed, stored, and consumed across the flow.

views.label: Risk View
views.description: Fragility, uncertainty, manual processes, and operational exposure.

overlays.label: PII / Sensitive Data
overlays.description: Highlights nodes and edges that contain, process, transmit, or store personally identifiable information.

overlays.label: Manual Processes
overlays.description: Highlights manual steps, spreadsheets, human workarounds, and manual data transfers.

overlays.label: Unknowns & Low Confidence
overlays.description: Highlights nodes and edges with medium or low confidence, open questions, and assumed details.

overlays.label: High Risk
overlays.description: Highlights elements with elevated risk — fragile integrations, manual dependencies, lack of ownership.

overlays.label: System of Record
overlays.description: Highlights authoritative sources for key data objects.

scenarios.label: Happy Path — Quote to Results
scenarios.description: Customer enters details, enrichment passes without discrepancy, quotes are distributed, results displayed.

scenarios.label: Claims Discrepancy — Customer Resolution
scenarios.description: Customer enters details, CUE returns undeclared claims, customer is prompted to resolve before quotes proceed.

evidence.label: Architecture walkthrough with Platform Engineering lead
evidence.excerpt: The backend orchestrates all enrichment calls in parallel before assembling the final quote request. Timeout is 3 seconds per provider.

evidence.label: CUE API integration specification
evidence.excerpt: Claims data is returned as a structured array of claim events linked to the policyholder's licence number.

evidence.label: Interview with Head of Commercial Partnerships
evidence.excerpt: Commission reconciliation is still done in Excel. We've been talking about automating it for two years but it keeps getting deprioritised.

evidence.label: Briefing with Product Manager on quote journey
evidence.excerpt: The discrepancy screen was added last year after we found that about 8% of customers under-declare claims. CUE catches most of them.
